prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39) and very high seasonality variance (0.55), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA best tracks the upward trend, THETA captures seasonality best, while DWT_svr achieves lowest overall RMSE. The early/late horizon specialists differ (ETS vs ONLY_DWT_rf), supporting DBA combination which handles heterogeneous model behaviors effectively.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "ONLY_DWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_svr", "ETS", "rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["catboost", "THETA", "ARIMA", "FT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3922,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39) and very high seasonality variance (0.55), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA best tracks the upward trend, THETA captures seasonality best, while DWT_svr achieves lowest overall RMSE. The early/late horizon specialists differ (ETS vs ONLY_DWT_rf), supporting DBA combination which handles heterogeneous model behaviors effectively."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for handling high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39). DWT_svr is overall RMSE champion (18231.6) while ETS and THETA are trend/seasonality champions respectively, supporting diverse method inclusion. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 uses recommended shrinkage knob. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 provides stability with small k given n_windows=3."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all strategies use past windows only or are baselines. Universe leaderboard candidates (poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 score 0.638, trimmed_mean_r0.05 score 0.641) have lower scores than current set (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3: 0.682, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35: 0.685, best_per_horizon_by_validation: 0.703, dba_combination: 0.711). No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). All candidate names are valid. n_windows=3 allows weighted strategies with learns_weights.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0", "trimmed_mean_r0.05"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Universe leaderboards show poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 (RMSE=17613) and trimmed_mean_r0.05 (RMSE=17829) significantly outperform current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 RMSE=18388). n_windows=3 is low, so adding diverse weighted/selection methods improves robustness. relative_spread_mean=0.12 < 0.25 threshold, so DBA not strictly needed. n_unique_winners=8 >= 3 supports keeping per-horizon selection candidates.", "changes": ["added poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 for best RMSE from universe", "added trimmed_mean_r0.05 as robust baseline alternative"], "when_good": "candidate set has diverse methods (selection, weighted, baseline) and n_unique_winners >= 3"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39) and very high seasonality variance (0.55), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA best tracks the upward trend, THETA captures seasonality best, while DWT_svr achieves lowest overall RMSE. The early/late horizon specialists differ (ETS vs ONLY_DWT_rf), supporting DBA combination which handles heterogeneous model behaviors effectively.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for handling high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.39). DWT_svr is overall RMSE champion (18231.6) while ETS and THETA are trend/seasonality champions respectively, supporting diverse method inclusion. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 uses recommended shrinkage knob. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 provides stability with small k given n_windows=3.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all strategies use past windows only or are baselines. Universe leaderboard candidates (poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 score 0.638, trimmed_mean_r0.05 score 0.641) have lower scores than current set (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3: 0.682, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35: 0.685, best_per_horizon_by_validation: 0.703, dba_combination: 0.711). No redundancy issues (no ≥4 same-type candidates). All candidate names are valid. n_windows=3 allows weighted strategies with learns_weights.

statistician.rationale: Universe leaderboards show poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 (RMSE=17613) and trimmed_mean_r0.05 (RMSE=17829) significantly outperform current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 RMSE=18388). n_windows=3 is low, so adding diverse weighted/selection methods improves robustness. relative_spread_mean=0.12 < 0.25 threshold, so DBA not strictly needed. n_unique_winners=8 >= 3 supports keeping per-horizon selection candidates.
changes: added poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 for best RMSE from universe
added trimmed_mean_r0.05 as robust baseline alternative
statistician.when_good: candidate set has diverse methods (selection, weighted, baseline) and n_unique_winners >= 3
